_doc: T510: GitHub Copilot CLI reads hook config from `.github/hooks/*.json` ({version:1, hooks:{...}}; camelCase event names; command hooks carry bash/powershell/command; `command` is the cross-platform fallback copied to both; preToolUse `deny` blocks the tool). Each native event routes to a thin canonical entrypoint `python .github/hooks/g-hk-on-<event>.py` which calls `g_hk_core.dispatch("<event>")`. The shared core `.github/hooks/g_hk_core.py` is byte-identical across every platform overlay and runs the per-event CONCERN_CHAIN (concern hooks self-gate on payload). Native->canonical map (g_hk_core.PLATFORM_EVENT_MAP['copilot']): sessionStart->session-start, sessionEnd->session-end, userPromptSubmitted->user-prompt-submit, preToolUse->tool-start, postToolUse->tool-end. Copilot has no stop event (graceful degradation). Hooks are agent-session scope only (NOT git hooks / CI). Schema verified against https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/hooks-configuration .
